notes: MEDIUM — InputProvider.jsx lines 121-124: Gamepad A button re-sets actionPending.current=true every rAF frame it is held, because the gamepad poll runs unconditionally without a 'not already pending' guard. The keyboard path correctly gates on !e.repeat, but the gamepad path does not. Result: holding A on a gamepad fires action=true every frame, not one pulse per press. Receipt claims 'Gamepad A button is also edge-triggered via the same pending mechanism' — this claim is incorrect. Fix: guard the gamepad set with `if (!actionPending.current)` (same pattern as keyboard repeat guard) so the pending flag is only armed on the rising edge. Same applies to GP_BTN_B / cancelPending. | MEDIUM — InputProvider.jsx: The rAF tick loop runs every frame unconditionally; navigator.getGamepads() is called and Array.from(pads).find(Boolean) is evaluated every frame regardless of whether any gamepad is connected. The worker's definition standard says 'Don't poll the gamepad if no pads are connected (check navigator.getGamepads().some(Boolean) first).' The worker chose an always-on loop and documented it as a design decision; the cost is minimal (O(4) array scan per frame), but it is a literal breach of the declared standard. Acceptable mitigation: add a cheap `connected` ref toggled by gamepadconnected/gamepaddisconnected events and skip the getGamepads() call when false. | LOW — TouchJoystick.jsx line 61: `const touch = e.touches[0]` declared in handleTouchStart but never read (the touch coordinates are not needed on start, only the element rect is used). Dead variable; remove it. | CRITICAL checks all PASS: scope is fully contained within src/renderer/game3d/input/ plus the permitted single edit to src/renderer/game3d/Game3D.jsx. Camera-switcher useEffect (1/2/3 keys) is intact and unchanged at Game3D.jsx lines 27-34. usePhysicsDebug() call and PhysicsWorld debug prop are intact at lines 25 and 44. KEY_MAP contains no entries for '1', '2', '3', 'p', or 'P'. No TypeScript files introduced. No package.json modification. useInput() return shape matches spec exactly: { moveX, moveY, action, cancel, debug, source }. Single useEffect in InputProvider with full keydown/keyup and rAF cleanup on unmount. F1 debug latch correct. TouchJoystick injects through context setTouch() callback, no own window listener stack. Receipt files_changed and exports_added are accurate.
